notes: Send all 6 starting villagers to sheep and keep making villagers nonstop.
Scout for sheep, gold, and your opponent.
Put 3 villagers on wood and build a Lumber Camp.
Build a House, and place a Farmhouse near your food so your farm economy starts strong.
Keep most villagers on food; transition onto Farms as the sheep run out.
Japanese farms are efficient — leaning on food keeps villagers producing.
Send 3 villagers to gold with a Mining Camp.
With about 400 food and 200 wood, age up to Feudal (Floating Gate for a safer boom).
Keep gathering through the age-up and decide your plan by scouting: economy, infantry, or mounted harass.
Build a Buddhist or Shinto Temple — its bonus supports whichever path you pick.
In Feudal, build a Barracks and start Samurai — they're durable and hit hard.
Add a Bannerman; its aura buffs nearby units in fights.
Take efficient fights around your Samurai's durability and the bannerman aura.
Keep adding Farms so your food never stalls while you produce army.
Pressure exposed villagers, but don't dive into mass ranged units — Samurai can be kited.
Pick up blacksmith upgrades and bank toward Castle Age.
In Castle Age, mix in Mounted Samurai and Handcannons, with a Mangonel for groups.
Use bannermen and Samurai durability to win the key fights and take the map.
